us-politics.headline: Trump Threatens Iran's Infrastructure, Rescues Downed Airman
us-politics.body:
The US military rescued a wounded F-15 pilot shot down over Iran in a high-risk operation that Trump publicly celebrated, while simultaneously warning Tehran that all of Iran could be "taken out in one night." Trump set a Tuesday deadline for Iran to meet US demands, then extended it - a move former National Security Advisor John Bolton says signals to Iran that Trump is not serious. Iran rejected a ceasefire proposal, with a former NATO commander noting the country still holds leverage.

Trump also floated charging tolls for Strait of Hormuz passage and brushed off questions about potential war crimes. Senator Bernie Sanders called Trump's threats the "ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual." Critics on both left and right are watching whether the deadline extension reflects diplomatic flexibility or weakens the US position.

sources.title: Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded
Former NATO commander on Iran rejecting ceasefire proposal: ‘They still have cards to play’
Live updates: Trump warns all of Iran ‘could be taken out in one night’ as he sticks to deadline
Bolton: Trump extending deadline shows Iran ‘he’s not really serious’
Trump says US will go after ‘leaker’ of info on downed pilot in Iran
Sanders on Trump Iran f-bomb threat: ‘Ravings of a dangerous and mentally unbalanced individual’
Trump touts airman rescue mission, boasts Iran could be 'taken out in 1 night'
‘God is good’: Inside the high-risk US mission to save a wounded airman shot down in Iran

us-politics.headline: Senate Battle Lines Form as GOP Locks In $342M War Chest
us-politics.body:
A Republican super PAC announced a $342 million investment in 2026 midterm Senate races, signalling an early and aggressive push to hold and expand the GOP's Senate majority. In Montana, Senator Steve Daines has handpicked Kurt Alme as his preferred successor, with Alme vowing to keep the seat red in what is expected to be a competitive race.

The spending announcement reflects Republican confidence but also the scale of resources needed to defend seats in contested states. Democrats have not yet announced comparable figures. Control of the Senate will shape the legislative agenda for the second half of Trump's term.

sources.title: Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races

us-politics.headline: Trump Administration Reshapes Education, Immigration and Legal Policy
us-politics.body:
The Education Department rescinded Title IX agreements with schools that had extended protections based on gender identity, reversing Biden-era policy. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth cut military education partnerships with Ivy League institutions, replacing them with Hillsdale College, a conservative school with close ties to the Republican movement.

On immigration, Arizona Republicans forced a probe into a county's policies limiting cooperation with ICE, putting the Democratic attorney general under pressure to act. Separately, proposed changes to the H2A agricultural visa program drew rare bipartisan opposition - conservatives worry about wage impacts on American workers, while liberals cite risks to migrant workers. The Supreme Court sided with Steve Bannon in a bid to dismiss his January 6 conviction, a significant legal development for a prominent Trump ally.

sources.title: Arizona Republicans force probe of county’s anti-ICE policies, putting Democrat AG on the clock
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity
Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers
Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in bid to dismiss Jan. 6 conviction
Raskin demands DOJ turn over details on settlement with Michael Flynn

us-politics.headline: Trump Accounts Launch, AP Cuts Staff, Late Show Era Ends
us-politics.body:
BNY Mellon will manage the newly created "Trump Accounts" ahead of the program's public launch, a Treasury-backed savings initiative targeting American families. Details on eligibility and structure remain limited. The program has drawn little congressional debate so far.

The Associated Press announced it is cutting dozens of staffers in a restructuring move, adding to broader pressures on legacy news organisations. CBS confirmed that Byron Allen's "Comics Unleashed" will take over Stephen Colbert's Late Show time slot following Colbert's departure, marking the end of a decade-long run for one of late-night television's most politically outspoken hosts.

sources.title: Associated Press cutting dozens of staffers
CBS announces what’s taking over Colbert’s ‘Late Show’ time slot
BNY to manage Trump Accounts ahead of program’s launch

au-politics.headline: Cyclone Maila bears down on Far North Queensland
au-politics.body:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is forecast to cross Far North Queensland, arriving just three weeks after Cyclone Narelle tore through the same region. Authorities are urging residents to prepare as the system intensifies offshore.

The back-to-back cyclone threat puts enormous pressure on communities still recovering from Narelle's damage. Emergency services and local councils are coordinating responses, though the exact landfall point remains uncertain as the system tracks south.

sources.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland
Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila could hit far north Queensland three weeks after Narelle tore through

au-politics.headline: First Nations voters questioned over identity at SA polling booths
au-politics.body:
First Nations voters in South Australia report being asked to verify their Indigenous identity at polling booths during the state's Voice to Parliament elections, with some describing the experience as humiliating. The question 'Are you sure?' was put to multiple voters, prompting calls for a formal review of electoral procedures.

Advocates argue the practice singles out Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander voters in a way that no other group faces. Electoral officials have not yet responded publicly to the complaints, but community leaders say the issue undermines trust in the process itself.

sources.title: 'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths

au-politics.headline: Wind farm conflict, cattle deaths and aged care failures test governments
au-politics.body:
Three separate accountability stories are running hard. A planned Tasmanian wind farm with links to minister Bridget Archer has drawn concern from local residents who question the conflict of interest. In Queensland, the state government has ruled out investigating luxury beef giant AACO after cattle died of thirst, with regulators notified only after the deaths. And in the ACT, a paraplegic woman named Mary has spent 19 months stranded in an inappropriate facility while waiting for an aged care bed.

Taken together, the cases point to recurring gaps between regulatory frameworks and real-world outcomes. Critics say each involves institutions that were slow to act, or chose not to.

sources.title: Planned wind farm linked to Tasmanian minister has some residents worried
A luxury beef giant's cattle died of thirst. Regulators were told later
Mary has fallen into an aged care 'black hole' and sees no way out

au-politics.headline: Iran rejects truce as Trump threatens military strike
au-politics.body:
Iran has rejected a ceasefire proposal and shows no sign of reopening the Strait of Hormuz, prompting US President Donald Trump to warn that Iran 'could be taken out in one night.' The threat escalates an already volatile standoff with direct implications for global oil supply and Australian energy costs.

Israeli settler violence in the West Bank is also intensifying against the backdrop of the Iran conflict, with analysts warning the two theatres are feeding each other. Australia has not yet issued a formal response to Trump's latest remarks.

sources.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Iran rejects truce deal
Israeli settler violence in the West Bank deepens against backdrop of Iran war
Live: Trump says Iran 'could be taken out in one night' if Strait of Hormuz is not reopened

world.headline: US and Israel Strike Iran as Hormuz Deadline Looms
world.body:
American and Israeli forces have intensified strikes on Iran, hitting residential areas, a top university, and military infrastructure. Defense Secretary Hegseth confirmed the campaign is escalating, with Trump warning that a deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is final. Iran is pushing a proposal to end the conflict, but Washington shows no sign of pausing.

Hezbollah and the Houthis have joined Iran in retaliatory strikes on Israel, widening the war across the region. A US airman was rescued from a remote part of Iran during the campaign. Trump publicly praised the operation at a White House Easter event, then threatened to jail a reporter who leaked details of it.

The conflict is hammering global energy markets. Asia is bearing the sharpest economic pain, while Libya's oil disputes are compounding European supply fears and Cairo is already dimming its lights to cope.

sources.title: Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel
Trump claims Iranians begging US to keep bombing Iran
Video: Trump threatens to jail reporter over Iran airman rescue leak
Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms
Video: Iran’s top university hit in US-Israeli strikes
Democrats blast Trump for Iran ‘war crimes’ threat; Republicans supportive
How Asia became ground zero for the oil crisis
Cairo faces darker nights as global energy prices continue to soar
Trump warns Hormuz deadline ‘final’ as Iran pushes proposal to end war
Trump praised airman’s rescue in Iran during White House Easter event
Libya’s oil disputes mirror Hormuz crisis, fuel European energy fears

world.headline: Gaza and Lebanon Under Fire as Civilian Toll Mounts
world.body:
Israeli strikes killed at least ten Palestinians near a school in Gaza, with clashes between Hamas and a rival militia adding to the death toll. Israel has also deepened its ground offensive in Lebanon, with renewed airstrikes pounding the country as Hezbollah fires back.

The dual fronts are stretching humanitarian conditions to a breaking point. In Gaza, aid workers have set up camps specifically to give children a temporary sense of safety amid relentless bombardment.

sources.title: Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
Gaza camp offers children a sense of safety

world.headline: Russia Jails Kursk Governor; Strikes Bury Child in Ukraine
world.body:
Russia has imprisoned the former governor of Kursk Oblast on corruption charges linked to the Ukrainian military incursion into the region last year. The arrest signals the Kremlin is holding officials accountable for security failures during the cross-border offensive.

Meanwhile, Russia ramped up missile and drone strikes on Ukrainian cities, with rescuers pulling a child from rubble in the aftermath. The strikes mark one of the heavier bombardments in recent weeks.

sources.title: Russia jails former Kursk governor in Ukraine incursion-linked graft probe
Child rescued from rubble after Russia ramps up strikes on Ukraine

world.headline: Supreme Court Clears Bannon; Artemis II Breaks Distance Record
world.body:
The US Supreme Court has cleared the way for Steve Bannon's contempt of Congress conviction to be dismissed, a significant legal victory for the Trump ally who has been pressing for the case's collapse since his release from prison.

On a brighter note, NASA's Artemis II crew broke the Apollo 13 record for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth, marking a milestone in the agency's push to return astronauts to deep space.

sources.title: US Supreme Court clears path for Steve Bannon criminal case dismissal
Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth
US Supreme Court paves way for dismissal of Steve Bannon conviction

ai.headline: Iran Threatens OpenAI's Stargate Data Center in UAE
ai.body:
Iran has issued threats against OpenAI's Stargate AI data center under construction in Abu Dhabi, raising security concerns for one of the most high-profile infrastructure bets in the AI industry. The threats come as the Stargate project - a joint venture backed by OpenAI, SoftBank, and others - pushes to build out massive compute capacity across the Middle East and beyond.

The timing is awkward for OpenAI, which this week also published a policy vision calling for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek to manage AI's economic disruption. Selling a benign future for AI is harder when your data centers are geopolitical targets.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek

ai.headline: AI Agents Are Crashing Wikipedia and the Broader Web
ai.body:
Wikipedia is fighting a growing wave of AI agents editing and querying its content at scale, and security researchers say it signals a wider problem. Bots trained to act autonomously are increasingly indistinguishable from human users, straining platforms that were never designed to handle them.

The pressure is real enough that a new benchmark called the Agent Reading Test has emerged specifically to measure whether an agent can comprehend content the way a human would. Meanwhile, tools like Freestyle are launching sandboxed environments for coding agents, and Reducto released Deep Extract, an agent-focused document parsing tool. The infrastructure for autonomous AI action is maturing fast. The guardrails are not.

sources.title: Wikipedia's AI agent row likely just the beginning of the bot-ocalypse
Agent Reading Test
Launch HN: Freestyle – Sandboxes for Coding Agents
Reducto releases Deep Extract

ai.headline: Offline AI Speech Tools Arrive From Google and Indie Developers
ai.body:
Google quietly shipped an offline-first AI dictation app for iOS, bringing on-device speech-to-text to a mainstream audience without the privacy tradeoffs of cloud processing. The move validates a trend already visible in the developer community - Ghost Pepper, a new open-source macOS tool, offers hold-to-talk local speech-to-text using the same logic: keep audio off the server entirely.

On-device AI inference is no longer a niche concern. As models shrink and hardware improves, the expectation that voice and language tools require a cloud connection is quietly eroding.

sources.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
Show HN: Ghost Pepper – 100% local hold-to-talk speech-to-text for macOS

ai.headline: macOS Hit by Two Separate Bugs With Serious Implications
ai.body:
Two distinct macOS vulnerabilities surfaced this week. One is a TCP networking bug that causes failures after a system has been running for 49 days - a classic integer overflow tied to millisecond counters that wraps around the 32-bit limit. The other is a privilege escalation flaw that lets an attacker gain root persistence by exploiting Safari inside macOS Recovery Mode, bypassing System Integrity Protection entirely.

The TCP bug is subtle and easy to miss in testing since most machines reboot before hitting the threshold. The Recovery Mode exploit is more alarming - it targets the one environment users trust to fix a compromised system.

sources.title: A time bomb in macOS TCP networking that detonates after 49 days
Root Persistence via macOS Recovery Mode Safari

btc.headline: Bitcoin Hovers Near $70K Amid Mixed Signals
btc.body:
Bitcoin climbed above $70,000 Monday before profit-taking pulled it back, leaving traders watching for a decisive break. Options markets are quietly pricing in significant downside risk, even as contrarian indicators suggest a potential bottom is forming. The tension is real: bulls and bears are both building cases.

Geopolitics added noise. Trump's ultimatum to Iran briefly rattled sentiment, then reports of ceasefire talks sparked a rally. Strategy kept buying regardless, adding 4,871 BTC for $330 million to bring its holdings near 767,000 BTC. Analysts at CoinTelegraph argue that Strategy's purchases are absorbing nearly three times new Bitcoin supply, which could push prices toward $110,000 if demand holds.

sources.title: Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?
Bitcoin climbs above $70,000 as more contrarian bottoming signs emerge
Bitcoin profit taking keeps BTC below $70K as Trump doubles down on Iran
Strategy adds $330M BTC as paper losses top $14.5B in Q1
Strategy added another 4,871 bitcoin for $330 million, with holdings nearing 767,000 BTC
Bitcoin has room to rally, but there's a catch
Bitcoin rallies on report of Iran ceasefire talks, Algorand extends gains
First real bull signal since 2025? Five things to know in Bitcoin this week

btc.headline: JPMorgan's Dimon Warns Blockchain Will Breed New Rivals
btc.body:
Jamie Dimon used JPMorgan's annual shareholder letter to flag blockchain and stablecoins as sources of serious competitive threat, naming the bank's own Kinexys platform as its response. He said the bank must move faster. That admission from Wall Street's most prominent skeptic marks a notable shift in tone.

The broader tokenization trend is gaining momentum elsewhere too. Bernstein analysts say Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could double its stock price. Bitmine's Ethereum treasury hit 4.8 million ETH as the company moves its listing to the NYSE. Institutional infrastructure is building fast.

sources.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon sees ‘new competitors’ from blockchain,  stablecoins
Figure Technology's tokenized credit platform could help double stock price: Bernstein
Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan must move faster as tokenization reshapes finance
Bitmine's ether treasury hits 4.8 million ETH as stock listing moves to NYSE
The future of institutional crypto runs through prime brokerages

btc.headline: US Crypto Legislation Moves Closer, Kalshi Wins Court Battle
btc.body:
A US Senate Banking Committee member confirmed an April timeline for crypto market structure legislation, adding pressure on Congress to deliver a framework that has stalled for years. The window is narrow and the politics remain complicated, but the signal from Capitol Hill is more concrete than usual.

Separately, a federal appeals court blocked New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports prediction markets, upholding a prior injunction against state enforcement. The ruling is a meaningful win for prediction market platforms fighting state-level crackdowns and could set a precedent as similar legal battles emerge elsewhere.

sources.title: US appeals court upholds preventing New Jersey enforcement against Kalshi
US Senate Banking panel member confirms April timeline for crypto market structure
Appeals court blocks New Jersey from shutting down Kalshi's sports markets

btc.headline: AI Deepfakes Target Crypto KYC as Aave Loses Risk Manager
btc.body:
A new AI-powered cybercrime tool is using deepfakes to bypass KYC systems at crypto exchanges and banks, raising fresh concerns about identity verification infrastructure across the industry. The threat is practical and already in use. Security teams are scrambling.

Inside DeFi, Aave lost Chaos Labs, one of its primary risk managers, amid a broader contributor exodus and governance disputes. The departure leaves a gap in Aave's risk oversight at a volatile moment for the protocol. Meanwhile, onchain perpetual DEX volumes have fallen for five consecutive months since an October peak, with Hyperliquid holding the largest share of a shrinking market.

sources.title: Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes
New AI cybercrime tool targets crypto, bank KYC systems via deepfakes
Onchain perp DEX volumes fall for five straight months after October peak

food.name: Miso-Glazed Aubergine (Nasu Dengaku)
food.description: Halved aubergines broiled until silky and caramelised under a sweet-savoury miso and mirin glaze
food.tip: Score the flesh in a crosshatch before glazing so the miso penetrates deeper and the edges char without burning

exercise.name: Monday Reset: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy shopping bags, a clear corridor or outdoor path
exercise.description: Alternate between farmer carries, suitcase carries (one side only), and overhead carries for 40-metre lengths with 90-second rests, building grip strength, core stability, and posture after a weekend of sitting

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity Over Time
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same thing — and philosophers from Hobbes to Parfit have weaponised it to interrogate whether you are the same person you were ten years ago.
learn.why: Derek Parfit's modern extension in 'Reasons and Persons' argues personal identity is less binary than we assume, which has quietly radical implications for how you think about self-improvement, regret, and moral responsibility
